regression.verdict: Domain effects SURVIVE controls. Category itself matters beyond microstructure.

Health.text: Health markets are well calibrated (Brier 0.000), consistent with wider bid-ask spreads (less liquidity), extreme base rate (100% yes), high price uncertainty (wide range).

Elections.text: Elections markets are well calibrated (Brier 0.000), consistent with high price uncertainty (wide range).

Social.text: Social markets are well calibrated (Brier 0.000), consistent with wider bid-ask spreads (less liquidity), high price uncertainty (wide range).

Science and Technology.text: Science and Technology markets are well calibrated (Brier 0.003), consistent with extreme base rate (14% yes), fewer price observations.

World.text: World markets are well calibrated (Brier 0.005), consistent with fewer price observations.

Entertainment.text: Entertainment markets are well calibrated (Brier 0.050), consistent with wider bid-ask spreads (less liquidity), high price uncertainty (wide range).

Politics.text: Politics markets are well calibrated (Brier 0.056), consistent with high price uncertainty (wide range).

Economics.text: Economics markets are well calibrated (Brier 0.068), consistent with wider bid-ask spreads (less liquidity), larger surprise price moves near resolution.
reasons: larger surprise price moves near resolution

Crypto.text: Crypto markets are moderately calibrated (Brier 0.085), consistent with larger surprise price moves near resolution, fewer price observations.
reasons: larger surprise price moves near resolution

Climate and Weather.text: Climate and Weather markets are moderately calibrated (Brier 0.087), consistent with fewer price observations.

Sports.text: Sports markets are moderately calibrated (Brier 0.127), consistent with larger surprise price moves near resolution, fewer price observations.
reasons: larger surprise price moves near resolution

Companies.text: Companies markets are poorly calibrated (Brier 0.168), consistent with wider bid-ask spreads (less liquidity), larger surprise price moves near resolution, high price uncertainty (wide range).
reasons: larger surprise price moves near resolution

Mentions.text: Mentions markets are poorly calibrated (Brier 0.244), consistent with wider bid-ask spreads (less liquidity), larger surprise price moves near resolution, fewer price observations.

Financials.text: Financials markets are poorly calibrated (Brier 0.245), consistent with wider bid-ask spreads (less liquidity), higher late-stage volatility, larger surprise price moves near resolution.
